title: Widen qualified ids to scoped addressing with project scope
description:
Generalize qualified-id addressing from the issue-scoped form to the full `<scope>/<self-id>` shape, where `scope` is either an issue short-id or `@` for the project. This lets project-level items (not tied to any one issue) be addressed alongside issue-scoped ones, completing the addressing model the rest of the work builds on. The id stays a pure projection, derived from the resolved scope and the parsed self-id and never persisted as a separate field, building on the issue-scope resolver and reusing `resolve_issue_id` (`crates/jit/src/storage/mod.rs:158`) and `Issue::short_id()` (`crates/jit/src/domain/types.rs:237`).

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: Resolving `@/<self-id>` (project scope) succeeds and returns the addressed item.
- [hard] REQ-02: Resolving `<issue-short-id>/<self-id>` succeeds and returns the addressed item.
- [hard] REQ-03: A self-id that appears more than once within a single scope is reported as a duplicate, rather than silently resolving to one of them.
- [hard] REQ-04: Self-id uniqueness is enforced per scope: the same self-id may exist under two different scopes without conflict.
- [hard] REQ-05: No new persisted id field is introduced; the qualified id remains derived from `short_id()` plus the parsed self-id (`cargo test` covers the resolve and degradation paths).
